_meta.note: Benchmark pool for earbuds_real. Content hash covers criteria and products only.

description: Real-world true-wireless earbuds benchmarks covering audiophile, commute, travel, business and budget use cases. Products and scores are derived from published specs and extensive user-review data.

criteria.name: price_to_value
criteria.label: Price to Value (feature density per dollar)

criteria.name: codec_support
criteria.label: Audio Codec Support (SBC / AAC / LDAC / aptX)

scenarios.name: Audiophile Android Commuter
scenarios.description: Heavy music listener on Android who needs LDAC, strong ANC for metro commutes, and studio-grade sound.

scenarios.name: Apple Ecosystem Daily Commuter
scenarios.description: iPhone user who wants seamless ecosystem integration, best-in-class transparency mode, and comfortable all-day wear.

scenarios.name: Premium Comfort Traveler
scenarios.description: Frequent flyer who values wearing comfort above all — long flights, extended airport waits, and all-day wearability.

scenarios.name: Value-Focused Audiophile
scenarios.description: Audiophile on a mid-range budget wanting excellent sound and LDAC support without spending $250+.

scenarios.name: Budget ANC Shopper
scenarios.description: Price-conscious buyer who wants solid active noise cancellation and long battery without spending more than $100.

scenarios.name: Business Professional — Call Quality
scenarios.description: Remote worker or business traveler who spends hours daily on calls and needs crystal-clear voice pickup and reliable call handling.

scenarios.name: Long-Haul Flight Companion
scenarios.description: Passenger on 10+ hour international flights who needs sustained comfort, reliable ANC, and quick transparency for announcements.

scenarios.name: Active Outdoor Athlete
scenarios.description: Runner or gym-goer who needs earbuds that stay put, survive sweat and rain, and work well for outdoor voice calls.

scenarios.name: Dedicated Music Listener (Home & Commute)
scenarios.description: Audiophile who primarily uses earbuds for high-quality music playback and wants the best sound signature with hi-res codec support.

scenarios.name: Budget Daily Driver
scenarios.description: Everyday user on a tight budget who just wants reliable earbuds with long battery life for music and podcasts all day.

scenarios.name: Podcast Creator & Remote Interviewer
scenarios.description: Content creator who records podcast interviews and voice content on the go — mic quality and call reliability are essential.

scenarios.name: Casual Daily Commuter
scenarios.description: Everyday commuter who wants good sound, reasonable ANC, and strong value — not willing to pay flagship prices.

scenarios.name: Premium — No Compromise
scenarios.description: Enthusiast willing to pay top dollar for the absolute best combination of sound, ANC, codec quality, and call performance.

scenarios.name: Sleep & Relaxation (Comfort-First)
scenarios.description: User who wears earbuds while falling asleep or meditating — needs the most comfortable fit that won't cause ear fatigue after hours.

scenarios.name: College Student — Dorm & Study
scenarios.description: College student who needs versatile earbuds for studying, casual listening, and gaming on a student budget.

human_judgments.name: Expert — Audiophile Android Power User
human_judgments.query: best wireless earbuds for audiophile Android users with LDAC
human_judgments.expert_rationale: For an Android audiophile, LDAC codec support is non-negotiable — it unlocks 990 kbps hi-res streaming from Tidal, Amazon Music HD, and Qobuz. Sony WF-1000XM5 dominates: LDAC + best-in-class ANC + audiophile-grade sound at 9/10. Nothing Ear (2) earns #2 with LDAC at $149 — exceptional value for the codec alone. Bose edges #3 with superior ANC and comfort despite lacking LDAC. Apple AirPods rank last here: AAC-only makes them unsuitable for serious Android audiophile use.
key_tradeoffs: Nothing Ear (2) delivers LDAC at nearly half the Sony price — the gap is ANC depth and build polish
Bose has superior physical comfort but its SBC/AAC-only codec disqualifies it from hi-res Android use
AirPods Pro 2 leads in ANC-to-comfort but AAC ceiling makes it a poor audiophile choice on Android

human_judgments.name: Expert — Apple Ecosystem Power User
human_judgments.query: best earbuds for iPhone users with seamless Apple integration
human_judgments.expert_rationale: AirPods Pro 2 is unequivocally the best choice for Apple users: Adaptive Audio, seamless iCloud device switching, Find My, Siri integration, and the best transparency mode ever shipped in an earbud. Bose takes #2 — its comfort is unmatched and it pairs reliably with iPhone with solid ANC, though lacking Apple-specific features. Sony is #3: strong sound and ANC, but the Apple-specific features that matter most (spatial audio in Apple content, Handoff, Live Listen) are AirPods-exclusive.
key_tradeoffs: Bose Immersive Audio vs AirPods Spatial Audio — both are excellent, but AirPods ties into Apple TV+ and Dolby Atmos ecosystem
Sony outperforms Bose on sound quality but its Android-first optimisations leave iPhone users missing features
Any non-AirPods choice sacrifices Adaptive Audio and seamless multi-device switching on Apple hardware

human_judgments.name: Expert — Remote Worker (All-Day Call Quality)
human_judgments.query: best wireless earbuds for remote workers who spend all day on calls
human_judgments.expert_rationale: Jabra has been the call-quality benchmark for years: its 6-microphone array with wind noise reduction and voice isolation produces the clearest pickup in any TWS earbud. IP55 rating means it survives outdoor walking calls year-round. AirPods Pro 2 earns #2 — Apple's H2 chip delivers crisp voice transmission and the stem mics excel in meetings. Sony is #3: eight-mic array and AI noise reduction are impressive, but Jabra's purpose-built business audio pipeline still leads in enterprise environments.
key_tradeoffs: AirPods Pro 2 wins on comfort for long calls but Jabra's mic array is purpose-built for voice isolation
Sony offers the best ANC for blocking office noise but its call quality trails Jabra in windy outdoor conditions
Jabra multipoint Bluetooth is more reliable than Sony for simultaneous phone + laptop connections

human_judgments.name: Expert — Budget Student ANC Shopper
human_judgments.query: best budget wireless earbuds with strong noise cancellation under $100
human_judgments.expert_rationale: Anker Soundcore Liberty 4 NC is the undisputed budget ANC champion: LDAC + 50-hour case battery + solid ANC for $80 is unprecedented value. It even achieves Hi-Res Audio certification. Nothing Ear (2) takes #2 — better sound signature and build at $149, but $70 more. Jabra Elite 5 rounds out #3: its IP55 durability and call quality make it excellent for students who commute in all weather, and the street price often drops below $100. Premium options (Sony, AirPods, Bose) are simply too expensive for the student use case.
key_tradeoffs: Nothing Ear (2) sounds better and looks cooler but costs nearly double the Anker
Jabra Elite 5 is more durable and has much better call quality — worth paying more for active campus life
Anker's LDAC at $80 is the standout feature — audiophile-grade codec at a fraction of premium prices

human_judgments.name: Expert — Premium Comfort Traveler
human_judgments.query: best earbuds for long-haul travel comfort and noise cancellation
human_judgments.expert_rationale: For long-haul travel comfort, Bose is in a class of its own. The StayHear Max tips distribute pressure across the outer ear rather than inserting deep into the canal — the only earbud that remains genuinely comfortable after 10+ hours. AirPods Pro 2 is #2: excellent H2-chip ANC, best transparency for airport announcements, and lightweight. Sony edges #3: best sound and equal ANC to AirPods but the larger shell causes fatigue over extended wear. Price is irrelevant for the traveler use case — comfort and silence are the non-negotiables.
key_tradeoffs: Sony has better codec support and sound quality but its larger housing causes noticeable fatigue past hour 6
AirPods Pro 2 leads in transparency mode — critical for hearing gate announcements without removing earbuds
Bose Immersive Audio spatial feature adds an enjoyable cinematic feel for movies on long-haul flights

human_judgments.name: Expert — Budget Audiophile (Android Mid-Range)
human_judgments.query: best wireless earbuds for audiophiles on a mid-range budget with Android
human_judgments.expert_rationale: Nothing Ear (2) is the clear winner for a budget audiophile: flat, reference-tuned sound signature, LDAC/LHDC codec support, 11-band custom EQ in the app, and a transparent design aesthetic — all for $149. Anker Soundcore Liberty 4 NC takes #2: its LDAC at $80 is extraordinary value and the 50-hour battery outlasts everything else in this segment. Sony WF-1000XM5 is #3: best overall sound quality of any TWS earbud, but at $280 it stretches the 'mid-range budget' premise — included for listeners who can stretch.
key_tradeoffs: Anker delivers LDAC at half the Nothing price but its sound tuning is more consumer V-shaped, less reference-accurate
Sony is technically superior to Nothing in sound quality but the $130 premium is hard to justify for budget-focused buyers
Nothing Ear (2) strikes the best balance: audiophile codec + tuning + app features at a sub-$150 price point
